name: implementing-code
description: Implements code changes and creates commits. Triggered when: implementation tasks, code changes, feature additions, bug fixes.
capabilities: Investigate related code using Glob and Grep
Create commits using Conventional Commits format
author: 1gy
securitySummary: This skill is a prompt-based definition file with no executable code. All static findings are false positives: the 'Hardcoded URL' and 'Hidden file access' findings refer to a source_url metadata field; 'C2 keywords' and 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' findings incorrectly flag hexadecimal hashes and plain text commit messages; 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' findings document standard git commit commands. The skill uses only Read, Write, Edit, Glob, Grep, and git commands for safe local development operations.
valueStatement: Need to implement features or fix bugs. This skill provides a structured workflow for investigating code, making changes, and creating conventional git commits.
limitations: Does not push commits to remote repositories
Does not create branches or pull requests
Requires git to be initialized in the project

useCases.title: Feature implementation
useCases.description: Add new features following project conventions and create properly formatted commits

useCases.title: Bug fixes
useCases.description: Investigate existing code and implement bug fixes with clear commit messages

useCases.title: Code refactoring
useCases.description: Refactor code while maintaining consistency and creating descriptive commits

promptTemplates.title: Basic implementation
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the implementing-code skill to add user authentication with email and password. Deliverable: Login form and validation.

promptTemplates.title: Bug fix
promptTemplates.prompt: Use implementing-code to fix the memory leak in the data processing module. Deliverable: Pass all existing tests.

promptTemplates.title: Refactoring
promptTemplates.prompt: Use implementing-code to refactor the utils module to use TypeScript types. Deliverable: Type-safe functions.

promptTemplates.title: Complex feature
promptTemplates.prompt: Use implementing-code to implement the dashboard with charts and data tables. Deliverable: Complete dashboard view with mock data.

bestPractices: Provide clear deliverables with specific acceptance criteria
Review existing code patterns before implementing
Use conventional commit format for clear changelog history
antiPatterns: Providing vague deliverables without clear scope
Making changes without investigating existing patterns

faq.question: Does this skill push commits to remote?
faq.answer: No, this skill only creates local commits. Use a separate skill or command to push.

faq.question: What commit formats are supported?
faq.answer: Uses Conventional Commits format: feat, fix, docs, refactor, test, chore.

faq.question: Can I use this with any git provider?
faq.answer: Yes, works with GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket, and any git hosting service.

faq.question: Is my code safe when using this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, changes are made locally. No data is sent to external services.

faq.question: What if multiple changes conflict?
faq.answer: The skill detects parallel execution and reports conflicts to the manager.

faq.question: How is this different from manual git commands?
faq.answer: Provides structured workflow with investigation, implementation, and proper commit messages.
